description:
Backlog anchor: `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/mastra-parity-backlog.md` → **Tier 1 / section 5. Completion markers and completed-observation lifecycle**.

Goal: define how planned / active / blocked / completed / abandoned-or-superseded work is represented so those states survive observation and reflection instead of living only as vague prose.

Relevant code paths:
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/types.ts`
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/state.ts`
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/prompts.ts`
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/agents.ts`
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/engine.ts`

Worker notes:
- it is fine if the durable representation is textual markers, lightweight structured metadata, or a hybrid; avoid overdesign
- preserve the current single-session OM model
- keep human-readable observations legible in the injected block

Validation should include tests under `packages/pi-om-extension/src/__tests__` that show lifecycle states do not collapse back into undifferentiated summaries.
title: Add explicit lifecycle markers for planned, active, blocked, completed, and abandoned work
